pr.title: Split pad.s to controller and rumble based on later map files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Split the former asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pad.s assembly unit into controller.s and a new rumble.s, using later map-file evidence for the module boundary. The controller file keeps the input/controller range through HSD_PadGetRawQueueCount at 0x8037808C, while rumble.s begins at HSD_PadRumbleOn at 0x80378090 and carries the following rumble-related assembly block through 0x80378A30. obj_files.mk was updated to build controller.o and rumble.o in place of pad.o.

key_files.role: renamed from pad.s; retains the controller portion and updates the .text range comment to 0x8037699C-0x8037808C
new assembly file containing the rumble block beginning with HSD_PadRumbleOn at 0x80378090
replaces pad.o with controller.o and rumble.o in TEXT_O_FILES

what_changed: Renamed asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pad.s to asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s with a reported 70% similarity.

Removed a 715-line tail block from controller.s starting at HSD_PadRumbleOn and continuing through anonymous address-named functions such as func_803780DC, func_80378128, func_80378560, func_803786F0, and func_80378828.
Added asm/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.s containing that removed 715-line block as a separate assembly translation unit.
Changed controller.s section range comment from the broad 0x80005940-0x803B7240 placeholder to 0x8037699C-0x8037808C.
Added rumble.s section range comment as 0x80378090-0x80378A30.
Updated obj_files.mk so the build uses asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.o followed by asm/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.o instead of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/pad.o.
Adjusted whitespace/alignment around the nearby video.o, controller.o, and rumble.o entries in obj_files.mk.
smart_moves: Used later map files as external evidence for the split instead of guessing subsystem boundaries from symbol names alone.
Kept the moved assembly text essentially intact, minimizing risk to matching while improving repository organization.
Preserved address continuity: controller.s ends at 0x8037808C and rumble.s starts immediately after at 0x80378090.
Placed controller.o before rumble.o in obj_files.mk, matching the address order implied by the original contiguous pad.s block.
Did not over-name unknown functions during the reorganization; address-based names such as func_803780DC were left unchanged where no stronger evidence was shown.
decomp_lessons: Later-version or later-map-file module names can be used to split large placeholder assembly files into more accurate source units before full C decompilation.
When splitting an assembly file, update both the file paths and the object list in the same PR so the linker layout remains coherent.
A safe organizational split should preserve instruction text, symbol labels, and relative order; the main intended effect should be file/module layout, not code semantics.
Section range comments are useful documentation for future decomp work because they make the expected address coverage of each asm file explicit.
Do not rename unknown functions merely because they moved into a better-named file; subsystem placement is useful evidence, but not always enough for function-level names.
naming_conventions: The old broad file name pad.s was replaced by controller.s for the controller/input portion.
The separated rumble-specific file is named rumble.s, matching the PR title and the later-map-file module boundary.
Exported HSD_Pad-prefixed names were preserved, including HSD_PadGetRawQueueCount in controller.s and HSD_PadRumbleOn in rumble.s.
Unknown functions retained address-based names such as func_803780DC and func_80378828 rather than receiving speculative rumble names.
Build objects mirror source file names: controller.s becomes controller.o and rumble.s becomes rumble.o.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved a contiguous tail block from the renamed controller.s into a new rumble.s file without evidence of instruction-level edits.
Used .section .text address comments to document each split range: controller 0x8037699C-0x8037808C and rumble 0x80378090-0x80378A30.
Kept .include "macros.inc" at the top of the new rumble.s assembly file.
Maintained object-file ordering in obj_files.mk to match the original text order: controller.o immediately before rumble.o.
The split point is anchored on the global symbol HSD_PadRumbleOn at address 0x80378090.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s or asm/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.s.
Search for map-file-derived splits of other sysdolphin baselib assembly files.
Look for later naming of func_803780DC, func_80378128, func_80378560, func_803786F0, and func_80378828.
Check whether future build layout changes preserve controller.o before rumble.o.
